evals.name: heal-panel-debugging
evals.prompt: I'm testing my keep app's cake page at /docs/tables and the `enable` step keeps failing — the response body is just {"message":"not-tracked"} and the step's dot turned yellow with a ⚠ instead of the usual red. What does the yellow ⚠ mean, and what's the fastest way to get the chain green again without digging through the server source?
evals.expected_output: Explains the yellow ⚠ as a failed step with heal suggestions, points at the heal panel's one-click rule fixes (run the prerequisite tracking/enable step, pick an existing tableName from captures) and Ask Claude / POST /docs/_heal as the escalation.
assertions: Explains the yellow ⚠ dot as a failed step for which the heal rules produced suggestions (not a generic warning state)
Points the user at the heal panel's one-click fixes — e.g. running the prerequisite step that tracks/enables the table, or picking a tableName that exists from captured outputs
Mentions 'Ask Claude' / POST /docs/_heal as the escalation path when the rule fixes don't cover it
Does not invent UI elements, endpoints, or env vars that the skill does not document

evals.name: teardown-flow-safety
evals.prompt: my keep module has a 'teardown' flow that drops the mirror tables. twice now ive hit Run all in order while testing the happy path and it nuked my test data lol. how do i make sure teardown steps never run unless i really mean it? also after i fix a failing step in the middle is there a way to rerun just from that step instead of the whole chain
evals.expected_output: States that the cake's flow selector defaults to the untagged-only 'main' walk so flow-tagged teardown steps are opt-in, mentions per-step skip toggles, and answers the second question with 'run from here'.
assertions: States that the cake's flow selector defaults to 'main' — the untagged-only walk — so steps tagged with a flow (like teardown) do not run under Run all unless that flow is selected
Advises keeping/putting the destructive steps in a named flow (flows: 'teardown') rather than deleting them or hacking around the runner
Mentions the per-step skip toggle and that a skipped step's state stays parked
Answers the second question with 'run from here' — clears that step and everything after it, then reruns from there

evals.name: wire-private-claude-healer
evals.prompt: Set up our keep server so that when a cake step fails I can click 'Ask Claude' and get a diagnosis. We already run a private-claude service at https://tools.monsterrg.dev/private-claude with a bearer token. Tell me exactly what to configure on the keep server, what endpoint the cake UI calls, what the request/response looks like, and what the security model is.
evals.expected_output: Names PRIVATE_CLAUDE_URL and PRIVATE_CLAUDE_TOKEN, identifies POST /docs/_heal, describes the failure-bundle + process-graph forward to /v1/prompt with a JSON schema and the structured verdict (diagnosis + suggestion kinds), and states localhost-only/403, 503 unconfigured, 502 upstream.
assertions: Names PRIVATE_CLAUDE_URL as the env var to set on the keep server, and PRIVATE_CLAUDE_TOKEN for the bearer token
Identifies POST /docs/_heal as the endpoint the cake UI calls
Describes the structured verdict: a diagnosis plus suggestions with kinds such as set-input / run-step-first / edit-body (machine-applicable) and switch-flow / set-env / explain (advice)
States the security model: localhost-only (403 otherwise), 503 until PRIVATE_CLAUDE_URL is configured, upstream failures surfaced as 502
Mentions that the server forwards the failure bundle plus the whole composed process graph to the private-claude service's /v1/prompt with a JSON schema
